_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of LLM-specific security vulnerabilities, including the OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications (prompt injection, insecure output handling, training data poisoning, model theft, excessive agency).
comments: NICE task intersects with WIDAI SP domain. Knowledge SP-K-012 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RM-T-002 (RM, cosine: 0.539), SP-K-001 (SP, cosine: 0.506).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.2/10 (raw 0.518). Shared vocabulary: [vulnerabilities]. NICE-only terms [identify, threat] vs WIDAI-only terms [10, agency, applications, data, excessive, handling] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.854 (P=0.892, R=0.820). P exceeds R by 0.073 — the NICE element's 3 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 25 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the NICE element.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (100.0%). "Identify threat vulnerabilities" and "Knowledge of LLM-specific security vulnerabilities, including the OWASP Top 10 f" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.558 ≈ STS 0.518 (gap 0.040). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [vulnerabilities] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 3.7%. Shared tokens: [vulnerabilities]. NICE-only: [identify, threat]. WIDAI-only: [10, agency, applications, data, excessive, handling].

signals.detail: STS 0.518 barely clears classification threshold for Superset of. This match is fragile and may indicate insufficient KSA coverage for this competency area.
